current_suggestion.pitch: Your current long-form interview channels (Lex, Rogan, Modern Wisdom) overlap heavily, but Chris Williamson consistently pulls guests in the intersection of evolutionary psychology, behavior change, and male psychology that none of your current channels own — figures like Geoffrey Miller, Rob Henderson, or Jonathan Haidt in contexts more focused on applied philosophy and mental models than politics. He steelmans the 'inner game' angle more rigorously than the hustle-culture adjacent content you're avoiding, with a philosophy-meets-data sensibility that bridges Stoicism and modern behavioral science. Format is long-form solo and interview, typically 60-120 minutes, with above-average follow-up questions and a willingness to sit in nuance.
